serverInfo.name: ibkr
serverInfo.title: IBKR MCP Server
serverInfo.description: Local Interactive Brokers MCP server for agentic trading desks: full market data for agents (real-time per the user's IBKR market-data subscriptions), portfolio context, and advisory risk checks through IB Gateway or TWS.

requirements: IB Gateway 10.37+ or TWS running locally
IBKR Pro account with TWS API access

tools.name: ibkr_status
tools.description: Daemon, gateway, account, and subsystem health for troubleshooting data freshness or connectivity.

tools.name: ibkr_trading_status
tools.description: Read-only local order-entry readiness: disabled, paper-ready, live-ready, or blocked, with pinned-session evidence and concrete blockers.

tools.name: ibkr_settings
tools.description: Read platform settings and observed state: feature enablement, trading mode/account/build capability, safety limits, and market-data quality; cannot change settings.

tools.name: ibkr_orders_open
tools.description: Read-only open-order lifecycle state for the connected broker account and mode; reports journal and broker-callback state only.

tools.name: ibkr_orders_history
tools.description: Read bounded local order-journal history for the current broker account/mode without placing, modifying, cancelling, or transmitting any broker order. Use for recent trade-review forensics when the user asks what locally journaled order lifecycle/fill callbacks occurred over a date range. This read-only tool does not place orders; it only reports local journal evidence. Results are bounded by grouped-order limit and per-order event_limit so callback-heavy trailing stops do not flood the context; inspect events_truncated/total_events_count before treating event samples as complete. This is a local daemon journal view only, not an IBKR Activity Statement, trade confirmation, execution report, commission ledger, closed-position ledger, or broker-grade historical audit; it may miss manual orders, other-client orders, and rows outside the selected account/mode; broker activity missed while the daemon was offline no longer strands open rows (the journal reconciles against broker open-order snapshots and closes absent rows as `closed_reconciled`), though per-event history for the offline window stays unavailable locally. For currently working orders use `ibkr_orders_open`; for one order's full audit use `ibkr_order_status`. Official broker history now flows into the local reconciliation surface when Flex ingestion is configured — ask the user to run `ibkr recon` — or ask them for an IBKR Activity Statement/Flex export directly.

event_limit.description: maximum lifecycle events returned per grouped order row; default 20, max 200. When truncated, rows carry events_truncated and total_events_count.

limit.description: maximum grouped order rows to return; default 50, max 500

since.description: optional inclusive lower boundary as YYYY-MM-DD UTC date or RFC3339 timestamp; default is 7 days before until

until.description: optional upper boundary as RFC3339 timestamp, or YYYY-MM-DD UTC date to include that whole UTC day; default is now

tools.name: ibkr_order_status
tools.description: Read-only lifecycle and audit events for one locally journaled order by order ref, IBKR order ID, or permanent ID.

id.description: order identifier to inspect: local order_ref such as ibkr-20260528-093000, IBKR order ID, or permanent ID

tools.name: ibkr_order_preview
tools.description: Draft a locally gated stock/ETF or single-leg option LMT, TRAIL, or TRAIL LIMIT order and mint a short-lived local preview token; cannot place, modify, cancel, or transmit a broker order.

action.description: order side; buy increases or closes short exposure, sell reduces/closes long exposure unless the local policy allows the opening effect

currency.description: optional contract currency override, such as USD or EUR

exchange.description: optional IBKR exchange or venue override, such as SMART or IBIS

expiry.description: option expiry as YYYYMMDD. Required for sec_type OPT.

initial_stop_price.description: optional initial trail stop price. Omit to bind the stop from fresh bid/ask during preview.

limit.description: optional explicit LMT price. Do not send with TRAIL or TRAIL LIMIT; use limit_offset for TRAIL LIMIT.

limit_offset.description: TRAIL LIMIT offset from the dynamic stop. Required for TRAIL LIMIT and rejected for plain TRAIL.

market.description: optional stock routing shortcut; omit or use "us" for SMART/USD, use "de" for German/Xetra EUR equities via SMART with primary_exchange=IBIS

order_type.description: broker order type. Defaults to LMT, or TRAIL/TRAIL LIMIT when trail fields are supplied.

outside_rth.description: whether the draft allows outside regular trading hours. Default false; option protection previews should keep this false.

primary_exchange.description: optional IBKR primary-exchange hint when routing through SMART, such as IBIS for German/Xetra listings

quantity.description: share or option-contract quantity; must be positive

replace_id.description: optional existing open order ref, order ID, or permanent ID to preview a replacement draft

right.description: option right. Required for sec_type OPT.

sec_type.description: security type. Defaults to STK unless option fields are present.

strategy.description: pricing strategy. Defaults to patient-limit for LMT and broker-trail for TRAIL/TRAIL LIMIT.

strike.description: option strike. Required for sec_type OPT.

tif.description: time in force. DAY (default) expires at the session close; GTC persists until filled or cancelled and is accepted for TRAIL and TRAIL LIMIT orders only.

trail_offset_type.description: trail offset unit. Usually omit and let trailing_percent/trailing_amount choose it.

trailing_amount.description: absolute broker trail amount in the contract currency.

trailing_percent.description: IBKR trailing percent in percent units: 2 means 2%, 0.50 means 0.50%.

trigger_method.description: IBKR stop trigger method for TRAIL/TRAIL LIMIT drafts; omit for the daemon default. Stock/ETF protective trails default to 2 (LAST). Useful values include 1 double bid/ask, 2 last, 3 double last, 4 bid/ask, 7 last or bid/ask, 8 midpoint.

tools.name: ibkr_account
tools.description: Account-level net liquidation, buying power, cash, margin, daily P&L, and currency exposure.

tools.name: ibkr_positions
tools.description: Open positions, option legs, Greeks, quote context, and exposure grouped by underlying.

symbol.description: filter to a single underlying symbol (case-insensitive)

type.description: filter to stock or option positions

view.description: response shape: full returns existing stocks/options/by_underlying detail plus protection_coverage; risk returns compact portfolio aggregates, top exposures, option-health counts, protection coverage, and flagged option legs

tools.name: ibkr_quote
tools.description: Snapshot stock and ETF quotes with close context, freshness, spread, liquidity, and session warnings.

currency.description: optional ISO currency override for stocks, e.g. USD or EUR

exchange.description: optional IBKR exchange/venue override for stocks, e.g. SMART or IBIS; omit unless the default market route fails

market.description: optional stock routing shortcut; omit or use "us" for SMART/USD, use "de" for German/Xetra EUR equities via SMART with primary_exchange=IBIS

primary_exchange.description: optional IBKR primary-exchange hint when routing a stock through SMART, e.g. NASDAQ or IBIS

symbols.description: ticker symbols, e.g. ["AAPL","MSFT"] or ["MBG"] with market="de"

tools.name: ibkr_watch
tools.description: Saved local watchlist symbols with optional enriched quotes and held-position context.

include_positions.description: when include_quotes is true, attach compact held-stock context where available; default true

include_quotes.description: return enriched quote rows for saved symbols; default true. Set false only for the daemon-reachable list-only symbol inventory

timeout_ms.description: per-symbol quote timeout when include_quotes is true; default 5000 ms

tools.name: ibkr_calendar
tools.description: Official market-session calendars for supported US equity, US option, and Xetra markets.

date.description: optional local market date YYYY-MM-DD; omit to use now

days.description: number of calendar days to include in sessions (default 14, capped at 400)

market.description: which official calendar to query: us/us-equity for U.S. stocks and ETFs, us-options for U.S. listed options regular sessions, de/de-xetra for Xetra cash equities

tools.name: ibkr_chain
tools.description: Option expiries, implied volatility, expected move, strike grids, deltas, open interest, and liquidity gates.

all_expiries.description: when listing expiries, return every listed date (default: nearest 12 with IV)

expiry.description: expiry date YYYY-MM-DD; omit to list available expiries

max_dte.description: expiry-list filter: maximum calendar days to expiration, applied before IV fan-out

min_dte.description: expiry-list filter: minimum calendar days to expiration, applied before IV fan-out; useful for 3-6 month option screening

no_iv.description: when listing expiries, skip ATM IV (faster)

require_live_iv.description: expiry-list preflight guard; when true, skip slow IV fan-out outside U.S. option regular hours and return warning_details code live_option_iv_unavailable

target_dte.description: expiry-list filter: return the listed expiry closest to this calendar DTE, after min/max DTE filters when present

trading_class.description: exact-expiry SPX class selector; omit for auto-selection from IBKR classed sec-def data

width.description: strikes ATM ± this count (default 5; 0 returns ATM only)

tools.name: ibkr_history
tools.description: Daily equity or ETF OHLCV history for lookback, range, and moving-average analysis.

days.description: calendar-day lookback (default 90); the returned row count is smaller because non-trading days are skipped

tools.name: ibkr_technical
tools.description: One-call trend, moving-average, relative-strength, ATR, and liquidity screen for stocks and ETFs.

currency.description: optional ISO currency override for symbols, e.g. USD or EUR

exchange.description: optional IBKR exchange override for symbols, e.g. SMART or IBIS

lookback_days.description: calendar-day history lookback; default 420, enough for 200-DMA and 126 trading-bar returns

market.description: optional route for symbols, not the benchmark; omit/use us for SMART/USD, use de for Xetra/IBIS EUR equities

primary_exchange.description: optional primary-exchange hint for symbols, e.g. ARCA for ETFs or IBIS for Xetra

tools.name: ibkr_market_events
tools.description: Observed market-event flags for held or requested symbols: borrow tightness, extreme borrow fee, Reg SHO threshold membership, LULD and regulatory halts.

symbol.description: optional single symbol or comma-separated symbols; equivalent to symbols for simple calls

symbols.description: optional stock/ETF symbols to evaluate, e.g. ["AAPL","GME"]; omit to use held underlyings from the daemon positions snapshot

tools.name: ibkr_scan
tools.description: Configured or ad-hoc IBKR market scanners with enriched quote and liquidity context.

exchange.description: ad-hoc locationCode (e.g. "STK.US.MAJOR" or "STK.EU.IBIS") — required with `type` when no `preset` is given

exclude_penny.description: drop enriched rows below $5, equivalent to min_price at least 5

instrument.description: IBKR scanner instrument for ad-hoc scans; defaults to STK for US stocks, use STOCK.EU for European stock locations such as STK.EU.IBIS

limit.description: max rows; preset default when omitted; ad-hoc capped at 50

min_dollar_volume.description: drop enriched rows whose last×volume dollar-volume is below this value; rows without last or volume fail this filter

min_price.description: drop enriched rows whose last price is below this value; rows without last price fail this filter

min_volume.description: drop enriched rows whose current share volume is below this value; rows without volume fail this filter

preset.description: preset name from `ibkr_scan` with no args (e.g. "top-movers"); omit for ad-hoc or list mode

require_live.description: drop rows whose quote context is off-hours, stale, previous-close-only, or otherwise not a usable live quote

type.description: ad-hoc scanCode (e.g. "HIGH_LAST_VS_EMA50", "HIGH_VS_52W_HL", "MOST_ACTIVE_USD") — required with `exchange` when no `preset` is given

tools.name: ibkr_scan_params
tools.description: Scanner catalog discovery for gateway-supported scan codes, locations, and instruments.

include_raw_xml.description: include the gateway's raw XML payload (~200 KB); default false

instrument.description: filter scan_types to those valid for this instrument (e.g. "STK", "STOCK.EU", "OPT", "ETF"); empty returns all

tools.name: ibkr_breadth
tools.description: S&P 500 breadth readings for participation, moving-average, and new-high/new-low context.

timeout_ms.description: per-snapshot wait budget when the engine has a fresh value but the wire envelope is still being assembled (default 5000 ms); does not affect the multi-minute cold-start fan-out

tools.name: ibkr_gamma
tools.description: SPY, SPX, or combined dealer-gamma market-structure snapshot and zero-gamma regime hint.

force.description: diagnostics-only: start a fresh refresh; when a good cached value is already serving, keep serving it and promote the forced run only on success; default false

include_profiles.description: include full sweep profile arrays for charting; default false keeps the response compact for agents and tooling

scope.description: which underlying(s) to compute: 'spy+spx' (default request; SPX/SPXW is canonical and SPY is added when fresh/rankable, so SPY throttling does not block a rankable SPX result); 'spx' (canonical SPX-only production signal); 'spy' (SPY-only proxy/context, not the canonical S&P dealer-gamma signal). Omit for the default view. Mirrors the CLI's --only flag.

wait_ms.description: block up to this many ms for the result; 0 (default) returns the current status immediately

tools.name: ibkr_regime
tools.description: Risk-regime dashboard across volatility, credit, funding, FX carry, dealer gamma, and breadth.

view.description: response shape: detail returns the existing compact regime snapshot (default); monitor returns posture, lifecycle, summary, source health, warnings, and compact indicator rows only

tools.name: ibkr_canary
tools.description: Portfolio-aware stress check for scheduled review, readiness, source health, and attention states.

view.description: response shape: full returns the existing canary evidence payload (default); alert returns compact monitor headline, source health, portfolio/market summaries including held_stress, protection_coverage, option health, hedge offset, warnings, and non-observe flags

tools.name: ibkr_rules
tools.description: Advisory 14-rule daily trading checklist evaluated daemon-side against the live book: per-name exposure cap (with disclosed lower-bound breaches under Greeks gaps), single option-line premium cap (hedge lines get their own higher tier), negative-cash sell-only mode, portfolio extrinsic (theta-rent) budget excluding classified hedge legs, expiry runway on long options, catalyst coverage vs earnings, short options spanning earnings (short calls act; short puts watch, act on assignment notional), pre-earnings size freeze, red-on-green-tape relative weakness, winner-trim into strength, green-day execution nudge, hedge-band integrity (act past twice the band top), exit discipline on long-option loss fences, and non-base-currency FX exposure (watch-only). Rules 3/4/12 thresholds are regime-conditional (calm/early_warning/confirmed sets from the latched regime lifecycle stage; a carried stage evaluates worse-of carried/calm, a never-seen stage uses calm thresholds — both disclosed in row notes). Use when the user asks "what should I fix today?", "which rules am I breaking?", "is my book within my own risk rules?", or wants a daily discipline review. Rows are ranked hardest-first (`ranked` indexes: act > watch > unknown, then base-currency impact); statuses are pass/info/watch/act/unknown/not_evaluated where `unknown` means an input was missing (positions pending, earnings unknown, Greeks gaps, FX report absent) — never treat unknown as pass; `observed_is_lower_bound` marks a breach proven from partial data ("at least X%"). `earnings[]` shows each name's next earnings date with source (fetched/override/unknown, with estimated and stale flags); `input_health[]` is the result-level gate (includes the regime_stage row). Advisory only: verdicts never block orders; order previews may carry matching advisory `rule_*` warnings. NOT for the market-regime × portfolio alert verdict (use `ibkr_canary`), NOT for executable protective-stop candidates (use `ibkr_proposals`), and NOT a data source for positions themselves (use `ibkr_positions`).

symbol.description: optional underlying symbol (case-insensitive) to narrow per-rule offender lists; portfolio verdicts are unaffected

tools.name: ibkr_proposals
tools.description: Read daemon-owned protection proposals (trailing stops, theta hygiene, risk reduction) with per-row blockers; never previews, submits, or transmits.

refresh.description: when true, ask the daemon to recompute proposals before returning; otherwise returns the latest daemon snapshot

show.description: when true, records a shown audit event for returned proposal rows

tools.name: ibkr_opportunities
tools.description: Read daemon-owned opportunities for existing positions. Use when the user asks whether ibkr sees mechanical portfolio opportunities, especially long option exercise candidates where exercise may beat selling the option bid or reduce an illiquid risk position. Option-exercise rows include `post_exercise_risk`: before/after underlying share exposure, whether exercise opens/increases/flips/reduces/closes risk, current protection coverage state when available, and whether a protection review is needed after exercise. That context is advisory and does not authorize exercise. This tool can return the latest snapshot or request a refresh, but it is read-only: it does NOT preview exercise, submit exercise, place, modify, cancel, transmit, or expose submit-capable tokens. For holdings and current protection coverage use `ibkr_positions`; for candidate protective stops use `ibkr_proposals`; for local broker-write readiness use `ibkr_trading_status`.

refresh.description: when true, ask the daemon to recompute opportunities before returning; otherwise returns the latest daemon snapshot

show.description: when true, records a shown audit event for returned opportunity rows

tools.name: ibkr_size
tools.description: Fixed-fractional position sizing math against live net liquidation value; never an order ticket.

entry.description: planned entry price per share, quote currency

fx.description: quote-currency units per 1 base-currency unit (default 1.0 for same-currency trades)

lot.description: round shares down to this multiple (default 1; use 100 for one option contract's worth of stock)

stop.description: planned stop price per share, quote currency

symbol.description: ticker the trade plan applies to (for reporting only)

target.description: optional take-profit price; when set, response includes r (reward:risk multiple) and breakeven_win_rate

resources.description: Read or subscribe to coalesced stock and ETF quote updates.

safety.summary: The MCP tool surface is preview-only in every release variant: ibkr_order_preview can draft an order and mint a local preview token, but no MCP tool can place, modify, cancel, or transmit a broker order. Standard downloads are read-only builds; each release also publishes a clearly marked ibkr-trading CLI tarball for explicit opt-in order management.

keywords: portfolio review with Claude and IBKR
